title: A New Interpretation of The Disappearance of Nerv Branch 2
display_title: A New Interpretation of The Disappearance of Nerv Branch 2
wikitext:
[]: A New Interpretation of The Disappearance of Nerv Branch 2             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Aug 08, 2008 2:13 am I have a hypothesis that I have no way to back up. First, the known facts. Episode 16 was the first to be changed from the original idea on short notice, as the staff felt that the conversation between Shinji and Leliel was corny and cliche. Thus, it was decided that Shinji would instead talk to himself, or at least talk to Leliel in the form of another Shinji. It is this episode in which the series really starts to take a more experimental direction. Episode 16, as it was made, is exactly as the Proposal describes with the exception of the fact that Shinji talks to himself instead of to Leliel. Episode 17, however, is completely different. When Reichu translated the Proposal, she wrote: Episode 17: Asuka's 1st date: Asuka goes to an amusement park for the first time. Misato's past. A romance comedy.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 08, 2008 3:10 am That would certainly be epic lulz if true, but it's probably overanalyzing. I mean, Anno was probably the one who made changes anyway, so it's not like he was bitching out some old men for not realizing HIS IDEAL. Still, it made me think.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Fri Aug 08, 2008 7:12 am I like it, even though it's as you said: there's no real way to back it up.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Aug 08, 2008 8:42 am In any pieces of work, the psychology of the person/people making it usually appears, which are not necessarily obvious. (Probably obvious point. How ironical.) Therefore, it may not be completely true, but to some extent, the statement could be agreed on.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 09, 2008 5:52 pm well I don't know: Eva-04's disappearance was always part of the story, ever since Proposal; but maybe Gendo's specific line about Seele was indeed a self-referential jab at how the series was changing....

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:10 am We just will not be completely sure. We can only make assumptions, and I think that a) Yes, the statement is correct b) Yes, but that is not everything or c) No, that is just poppycock. To summarise things up shortly. BUT, I can TRY contacting people to see about their opinion, or if there are any secrets, but I got better things to do with my time. Unless you people insist.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 10, 2008 12:34 pm Personally I think this is interesting whether or not there's actually any truth to this. Nice so far!

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun Aug 10, 2008 1:07 pm The Imperialist wrote: We just will not be completely sure. We can only make assumptions, and I think that a) Yes, the statement is correct b) Yes, but that is not everything or c) No, that is just poppycock.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 2:07 pm The Imperialist wrote: BUT, I can TRY contacting people to see about their opinion, or if there are any secrets, but I got better things to do with my time. Unless you people insist.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:41 pm NAveryW wrote: Oh yeah, and translating ANIMA. That should probably be the highest priority for anyone who knows moonspeak

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 10, 2008 11:35 pm I can translate, but I nned the sources to translate from. I can contact people, but that would require many international phone calls, and my parents do not like to rack up bills. And I don't really have the time, but... I will think about it.

Episode 16 was the first to be changed from the original idea on short notice, as the staff felt that the conversation between Shinji and Leliel was corny and cliche. Thus, it was decided that Shinji would instead talk to himself, or at least talk to Leliel in the form of another Shinji. It is this episode in which the series really starts to take a more experimental direction. Episode 16, as it was made, is exactly as the Proposal describes with the exception of the fact that Shinji talks to himself instead of to Leliel. Episode 17, however, is completely different. When Reichu translated the Proposal, she wrote: Episode 17: Asuka's 1st date: Asuka goes to an amusement park for the first time. Misato's past. A romance comedy.
